name: NT-proBNP–guided therapy for heart failure (mortality)
condition: Chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF)
summary: Pooling 6 randomized trials (2,129 HFrEF patients), titrating heart-failure medications to an NT-proBNP target rather than by clinical judgement was associated with lower all-cause mortality (pooled RR ≈0.80, 95% CI 0.67–0.97; I²=0%). The single largest and most recent trial (GUIDE-IT) was individually neutral.
description: A pooled analysis (Bajaj et al 2018, JACC research letter) of the 6 randomized trials that used N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) — not BNP — to guide up-titration of heart-failure therapy in HFrEF (LVEF <50%). Each trial's risk ratio for all-cause mortality is taken from the letter's Figure 1A and pooled live; the letter reports 1,060 patients in the NT-proBNP-guided arm and 1,069 in the standard-therapy arm.
interpretation: The pooled estimate is significant, but read it with the authors' own caution. Heterogeneity is nil (I²=0%), yet the trials differ in NP target, LVEF cutoff, and how much extra up-titration the guided arm actually received — and the benefit is thought to operate through achieving higher HF-medication doses, not the biomarker itself. The largest, most rigorous trial, GUIDE-IT (2017), was individually neutral (RR 0.86, 0.64–1.16) and stopped for futility; its neutral result may reflect that the standard arm reached comparable target doses. The authors frame the pooled benefit as 'suggestive' and explicitly warn against concluding there is 'evidence of absence' of benefit — i.e., this is hypothesis-generating, not practice-changing.
methodologyNotes: Each trial is entered as its published risk ratio and 95% CI from the letter's Figure 1A (all-cause mortality) and pooled live by random-effects (DerSimonian–Laird), recovering each study's standard error from its reported CI. This gives a live pooled RR of 0.81 (95% CI 0.67–0.97) — the confidence interval matches the letter's exactly, and the point estimate matches its 0.80 within rounding (the letter pooled raw event counts by Mantel-Haenszel, whereas we pool the reported log-risk-ratios). The letter does not report deaths or arm sizes per trial, only the pooled arm totals (1,060 NT-proBNP-guided vs 1,069 standard = 2,129); per-trial N here is each trial's HFrEF analysis size, reconstructed to sum to that reported total, and is used only for the patient count, not the effect (which comes entirely from the reported RR/CI). Christchurch Pilot's very wide CI (0.16; 0.02–1.20) correctly gives it near-zero pooled weight, matching the letter's 0.83%. The letter's Figure 1B (1-study-removed sensitivity analysis) is not encoded.

outcomes.label: All-cause mortality
outcomes.description: Death from any cause during trial follow-up (median 9–23 months across trials).

studies.author: Troughton
studies.citation: Troughton RW, Frampton CM, Yandle TG, Espiner EA, Nicholls MG, Richards AM. Treatment of heart failure guided by plasma aminoterminal brain natriuretic peptide (N-BNP) concentrations. Lancet. 2000;355(9210):1126-1130.
studies.notes: Small pilot; very wide CI and near-zero pooled weight (0.83%).

studies.author: TIME-CHF
studies.citation: Pfisterer M, Buser P, Rickli H, et al. BNP-guided vs symptom-guided heart failure therapy: the Trial of Intensified vs Standard Medical Therapy in Elderly Patients With Congestive Heart Failure (TIME-CHF) randomized trial. JAMA. 2009;301(4):383-392.

studies.author: Berger
studies.citation: Berger R, Moertl D, Peter S, et al. N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide-guided, intensive patient management in addition to multidisciplinary care in chronic heart failure: a 3-arm, prospective, randomized pilot study. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2010;55(7):645-653.
studies.population: Austria (Vienna); HFrEF (NT-proBNP-guided vs control arms of a 3-arm pilot)

studies.author: PRIMA
studies.citation: Eurlings LW, van Pol PE, Kok WE, et al. Management of chronic heart failure guided by individual N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide targets: results of the PRIMA (Can PRo-brain-natriuretic peptide guided therapy of chronic heart failure IMprove heart fAilure morbidity and mortality?) study. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2010;56(25):2090-2100.

studies.author: SIGNAL-HF
studies.citation: Persson H, Erntell H, Eriksson B, Johansson G, Swedberg K, Dahlström U. Improved pharmacological therapy of chronic heart failure in primary care: a randomized Study of NT-proBNP Guided Management of Heart Failure — SIGNAL-HF. Eur J Heart Fail. 2010;12(12):1300-1308.

studies.author: GUIDE-IT
studies.citation: Felker GM, Anstrom KJ, Adams KF, et al. Effect of natriuretic peptide-guided therapy on hospitalization or cardiovascular mortality in high-risk patients with heart failure and reduced ejection fraction: a randomized clinical trial (GUIDE-IT). JAMA. 2017;318(8):713-720.
studies.notes: Largest and most recent trial (38.0% weight); individually neutral, stopped for futility.
